it.title: Fit for 55: EU Climate Legislation Package Achieving 55% Emissions Reduction by 2030
it.full_brief:
# Fit for 55: EU Climate Legislation Package — Achieving 55% Emissions Reduction by 2030

**Regulatory Fact Document | Severity: ACTION REQUIRED | Format: regulatory_fact_document**

---

# Purpose and Scope of This Document

This brief synthesises the Fit for 55 legislative package as it applies to a freight forwarding workspace operating primarily by air, with road as the secondary mode and ocean tertiary. It covers the package's adopted components that directly or indirectly govern the workspace's operating costs, compliance obligations, and competitive positioning across EU and trans-EU trade lanes.

**Coverage conventions:**
- All component instruments addressed here have been formally adopted as of the date of this brief unless otherwise noted.
- The package is not a single regulation but a collection of more than a dozen distinct legislative instruments; each creates separate legal obligations. This brief addresses those components most operationally material to freight forwarding: EU ETS (aviation and maritime), ETS2 (road transport fuels), CBAM, ReFuelEU Aviation, FuelEU Maritime, and AFIR.
- Items requiring legal interpretation are labeled *Legal Confirmation Required:*
- Binding verbs used in describing regulatory provisions are grounded to source spans or labeled as analytical inferences.
- The workspace is referenced throughout as "the workspace" or by its operational role. No company or individual names appear.
- Source dates used: primary source published 14 July 2021 (COM(2021)550); corroborating sources range from April 2023 to April 2026.

---

# What This Regulation Is and Why It Applies to the Workspace

## The Package in Plain Language

The Fit for 55 package is a collection of EU legislative instruments originally proposed by the European Commission on 14 July 2021, designed to make all sectors of the EU economy consistent with a binding target of reducing net greenhouse gas emissions by at least 55% compared to 1990 levels by 2030. *Source: COM(2021) 550 final, European Commission, 14 July 2021. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:52021DC0550

The EU Climate Law (Regulation (EU) 2021/1119) "establishes a binding Union domestic reduction target for net greenhouse gas emissions (emissions after deduction of removals) of at least 55 % compared to 1990 levels by 2030." *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/956, European Parliament and Council, 10 May 2023 (recital 5). https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R0956

All fifteen component instruments listed on the European Commission's tracking page carry the status "Adopted" as of 31 October 2025. *Source: Fit for 55: Delivering on the proposals, European Commission, last updated 31 October 2025. https://commission.europa.eu/topics/climate-action/delivering-european-green-deal/fit-55-delivering-proposals_en

**Effective date (package-level):** The overarching Fit for 55 legislative package was initiated by Commission proposal on 14 July 2021. Individual instruments have varying entry-into-force dates; the earliest (ETS revisions) applied from 1 January 2024. *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target

**Jurisdictional scope:** The package applies across the EU-27, with ETS and CBAM mechanisms extending obligations to non-EU operators trading with or operating in the EU. *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/956, European Parliament and Council, 10 May 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R0956

## Why This Applies to the Workspace

The workspace operates as a freight forwarder across air (primary), road (secondary), and ocean (tertiary) modes on EU, transatlantic, and Asia-Pacific trade lanes. The Fit for 55 package directly touches the workspace's cost base through four vectors:

1. **Air mode:** ReFuelEU Aviation establishes SAF blend mandates on EU airports, increasing aviation fuel costs that carriers pass through to airfreight customers. EU ETS already applies to intra-EEA aviation.
2. **Road mode:** ETS2 (fully operational 2028) places a carbon price on road transport fuel suppliers, who will pass costs through to haulage rates. The workspace procures road freight on behalf of clients for pickup/delivery legs.
3. **Ocean mode:** EU ETS phase-in for maritime shipping (40% of verified emissions surrendered in 2024, rising to 100% in 2026) has been increasing ocean carrier surcharges on EU-touching voyages since 2024.
4. **Customs/trade role:** CBAM affects importers of covered goods (cement, aluminium, fertilisers, electricity, hydrogen, iron and steel) into the EU. *Legal Confirmation Required:* whether the workspace, when acting as customs agent or indirect representative for an importer of CBAM-covered goods, bears any procedural obligation under CBAM, or whether all obligations rest solely with the declarant importer of record, requires counsel review.

*Analytical inference:* For a workspace whose cargo verticals include high-value manufactured goods, fine art crating and mounting hardware, film and TV equipment with steel or aluminium components, or humanitarian cargo containing fertiliser precursors, CBAM-covered goods may appear in shipments, creating downstream documentation and cost implications even where the workspace itself is not the importer.

---

# Issues Requiring Immediate Action

**COST ALERT — ETS2 Road Freight Cost Build-Up Beginning 2026**
Fuel suppliers will be required to surrender ETS2 allowances from 2028, but early ETS2 allowance auctions are planned for 2026 to allow suppliers to begin hedging. *Source: CFP Energy, ETS2 Road Transport Overview, 29 October 2025. https://www.cfp.energy/en/insights/ets2-road-transport-overview Road haulage carriers are likely to begin embedding expected ETS2 cost into rate structures before the 2028 operational start. *Analytical inference:* Workspaces that use multi-year road freight contracts or spot-rate benchmarks should review contractual arrangements to understand whether carbon cost pass-through clauses exist or are absent — absent clauses will mean unexpected cost absorption or client disputes when ETS2-related surcharges arrive.
**Action:** Review road haulage contracts for carbon pass-through language; request from carriers their ETS2 pricing model for 2026 hedging period. **Who:** Operations and commercial leadership. **Why now:** ETS2 futures trading has already begun (first EU ETS2 futures trade executed on EEX in August 2025). *Source: CFP Energy, ETS2 Road Transport Overview, 29 October 2025. https://www.cfp.energy/en/insights/ets2-road-transport-overview Rate structures are being built before 2028 full operationalisation.

---

**COST ALERT — EU ETS Maritime Surcharge Now at 100% from 2026**
The ETS maritime obligation, phased in from 40% of verified emissions in 2024, reaches 100% of intra-EU voyage emissions and 50% of extra-EU voyage emissions in 2026. *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target
**Action:** Obtain from ocean carrier partners their ETS surcharge methodology for 2026 shipments on EU-touching lanes; validate that surcharges are consistent with 100%/50% obligation structure. **Who:** Ocean procurement and pricing teams. **Why now:** The 100% intra-EU obligation is in force from 1 January 2026.

---

**ACTION REQUIRED — CBAM Full Compliance Obligation from 1 January 2026**
"The CBAM Regulation will apply from 1 October 2023 (reporting obligation), however the full compliance obligation for importers will only apply from 1 January 2026." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target
"Starting in 2026, importers in sectors covered by CBAM (cement, aluminum, fertilizers, electricity, hydrogen, iron and steel, along with some precursors and downstream products) will be required to surrender newly created CBAM certificates equivalent to the embedded emissions of their products." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-mirrors-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target
**Action:** For shipments containing CBAM-covered goods, confirm with importers of record that they hold or are obtaining CBAM authorisation; review client agreements to establish that CBAM certificate cost and documentation obligations sit explicitly with the importer, not with the workspace. **Who:** Legal, compliance, and account management teams. **Why now:** The full obligation is active from 1 January 2026.

---

**MONITORING — ETS2 MRV Already Running; Re-check window Q1 2027**
Monitoring and reporting obligations under ETS2 for fuel suppliers started in 2025. *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU ETS 2 factsheet. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/eu-emissions-trading-system-buildings-and-road-transport-eu-ets-2 The workspace is not itself a regulated entity under ETS2 (fuel suppliers are the obligated parties upstream). *Analytical inference:* Monitoring re-check recommended Q1 2027 as the surrender obligation start date (2028) approaches and carrier surcharge structures solidify.

---

# How the Workspace Sits in the Compliance Chain

## EU ETS (Aviation)

EU ETS has applied to intra-EEA aviation since 2012. Aircraft operators are the obligated parties. *Analytical inference:* The workspace as freight forwarder purchasing airfreight capacity is not an ETS obligated entity for aviation; rather, the workspace faces the ETS cost as a pass-through in carrier rates. *Legal Confirmation Required:* whether any specific disclosure or reporting obligation arises for a freight forwarder acting as IATA-accredited agent in connection with ETS surcharges on airfreight AWBs requires counsel review.

## EU ETS (Maritime)

Vessel operators (shipowners and managers) are the obligated parties under maritime ETS. *Source: Latham & Watkins, EU Sustainability State of Play, 1 October 2025. https://www.lw.com/en/insights/eu-sustainability-state-of-play-eu-climate-law-and-energy-transition The workspace as NVOCC or forwarder purchasing ocean freight is not the obligated party; carriers pass ETS costs through bunker and emissions surcharges. *Legal Confirmation Required:* whether a forwarder acting as NVOCC (issuing its own B/L) acquires any ETS obligation as a consequence of that role, or whether the obligation remains solely with the vessel operator, requires counsel review.

## CBAM

"Importers in sectors covered by CBAM...will be required to surrender newly created CBAM certificates equivalent to the embedded emissions of their products." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target The obligated party is the importer. *Legal Confirmation Required:* the boundary between an importer's obligations and a customs broker or indirect representative's procedural obligations under CBAM must be confirmed with counsel, particularly when the workspace acts as indirect customs representative.

## ETS2 (Road Transport)

"It will be fuel suppliers, rather than end consumers such as households or car users, that will be required to monitor and report their emissions." *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en The workspace as a purchaser of road freight services is not a regulated entity under ETS2. *Analytical inference:* However, the workspace bears ETS2 costs indirectly as road carriers embed allowance costs in haulage rates.

## ReFuelEU Aviation and FuelEU Maritime

These regulations set blend obligations on aviation fuel suppliers and maritime operators respectively. The workspace is not the obligated entity. *Analytical inference:* Both regulations increase the operating cost of air and ocean carriers, which the workspace will experience through rate adjustments, particularly on EU routes.

## AFIR

AFIR establishes infrastructure obligations on EU Member States, ports, and TEN-T network operators. The workspace is not a regulated entity. *Analytical inference:* AFIR indirectly benefits the workspace by expanding shore power and alternative fuel refuelling points, reducing infrastructure barriers for carriers pursuing lower-emission operations.

---

# Authoritative Guidance Document Analysis

## EU Commission Communication COM(2021) 550

The Commission's 14 July 2021 communication describes the Fit for 55 package as "a set of interconnected proposals that together deliver our ambition" and characterises the policy mix as "a careful balance between pricing, targets, standards and support measures." *Source: COM(2021) 550 final, European Commission, 14 July 2021. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:52021DC0550 This framing is operationally significant: the package's components are designed to interact, meaning cost pressures arrive across modes simultaneously, not sequentially.

The communication explicitly identifies that "carbon pricing instruments...raise revenues that can be reinvested to address energy poverty and mobility challenges for the vulnerable." *Source: COM(2021) 550 final. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:52021DC0550 For a workspace purchasing road and air transport services, this means carriers face a carbon cost that is structurally embedded into EU policy design — not a temporary measure.

The communication describes the Social Climate Fund as providing "€72.2 billion in current prices for the period 2025-2032 in the EU budget from the new Emissions Trading System." *Source: COM(2021) 550 final. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:52021DC0550 This revenue recycling mechanism does not reduce the compliance cost for commercial freight; it is directed at vulnerable households and micro-enterprises.

## CBAM Regulation (EU) 2023/956

The regulation was published in the Official Journal on 16 May 2023 (OJ L 130, pp. 52–104). *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/956. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R0956 The CBAM phase-out of free ETS allocation for covered sectors runs "over a nine-year period (from 2026 to 2034)." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target This phased structure means the cost of importing CBAM-covered goods will rise incrementally, not spike in a single year.

The CBAM applies to "cement, aluminum, fertilizers, electricity, hydrogen, iron and steel, along with some precursors and downstream products." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target For the workspace, this list defines which shipments may carry CBAM-related documentation requirements imposed on the importer.

## CO2 Emission Standards for Cars and Vans (Regulation (EU) 2023/851)

The regulation amends Regulation (EU) 2019/631 "as regards strengthening the CO2 emission performance standards for new passenger cars and new light commercial vehicles in line with the Union's increased climate ambition." *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/851, European Parliament and Council, 19 April 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R0851 *Analytical inference:* For the workspace's road operations, this regulation accelerates fleet electrification by road carriers, which over time reduces ETS2 exposure per vehicle but increases short-term capital costs for carriers operating light commercial vans — the most common vehicle category for last-mile freight delivery on EU trade lanes.

## ETS2 Legal Framework

Directive (EU) 2023/959 "amending Directive 2003/87/EC...supplemented the existing emissions trading system at EU level with regulations for the EU ETS 2." *Source: DEHSt, EU ETS 2 reporting phase (2024-2027), last updated 14 April 2026. https://www.dehst.de/EN/Topics/nEHS/EU-ETS-2/reporting-phase-2024-2026/eu-ets2_node.html The ETS2 operates as an upstream system: "it is not the users of fossil fuels...who are obliged to participate, but those companies that place fuels on the market within the scope of EU ETS 2 (for example gas traders)." *Source: DEHSt, EU ETS 2 reporting phase (2024-2027). https://www.dehst.de/EN/Topics/nEHS/EU-ETS-2/reporting-phase-2024-2026/eu-ets2_node.html

## AFIR — Regulation 2023/1804

"AFIR Regulation 2023/1804 was published in the EU Official Journal on 22 September 2023. It entered into force on 12 October 2023." *Source: Bureau Veritas Marine & Offshore, Alternative Fuels Infrastructure Regulation. https://marine-offshore.bureauveritas.com/sustainability/fit-55/alternative-fuels-infrastructure-regulation Key deadlines under AFIR: 31 December 2024 deadline for submission of draft national policy frameworks by EU Member States; TEN-T maritime ports providing shore-side electricity for container and passenger ships over 5,000 GT as of January 2030. *Source: Bureau Veritas Marine & Offshore, Alternative Fuels Infrastructure Regulation. https://marine-offshore.bureauveritas.com/sustainability/fit-55/alternative-fuels-infrastructure-regulation

---

# Anticipated Authoritative Guidance and Pending Regulatory Events

**EU Climate Law 2040 Target Amendment — MONITORING**
In July 2025, the Commission proposed an amendment to the EU Climate Law establishing "an EU climate target for 2040 of a 90% reduction in net GHG emissions, compared to 1990 levels." The proposal has been submitted "to the European Parliament (Parliament) and the European Council (Council) for discussion and adoption under the ordinary legislative procedure." *Source: Latham & Watkins, EU Sustainability State of Play, 1 October 2025. https://www.lw.com/en/insights/eu-sustainability-state-of-play-eu-climate-law-and-energy-transition
*Analytical inference:* Adoption timeline under the ordinary legislative procedure typically runs 12–24 months from Commission proposal; the workspace should expect a legislative outcome in the 2026–2027 window. This amendment will set the legal baseline for a post-2030 regulatory tightening cycle, including further ETS cap reductions and potential new sectoral instruments.
**Re-check window: Q4 2026** (expected first readings in Parliament and Council).

---

**Carbon Removal Credit Integration into EU ETS — MONITORING**
"The Commission launched a public consultation in April 2025 to gather input on the integration of GHG removals...with a report expected by July 2026." *Source: Latham & Watkins, EU Sustainability State of Play, 1 October 2025. https://www.lw.com/en/insights/eu-sustainability-state-of-play-eu-climate-law-and-energy-transition The EU Carbon Removal Certification Framework was approved by the Council in November 2024 as a voluntary framework.
*Analytical inference:* If carbon removal credits are integrated into the EU ETS post-2030, carriers and forwarders operating on EU routes may have additional compliance flexibility — but this is speculative pending the July 2026 report and subsequent legislative steps.
**Re-check window: Q3 2026** (report due date per Commission announcement).

---

**ETS2 MSR Amendment — MONITORING**
The Commission proposed further changes to the ETS2 market stability reserve in November 2025 "to support a smooth start of the EU ETS 2 and accelerate early investments into sectoral decarbonization." These changes include "doubling the number of allowances to be injected if the carbon price exceeds EUR 45." "Member States and the European Parliament will have to approve those changes before they come into force." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU ETS 2 factsheet. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/eu-emissions-trading-system-buildings-and-road-transport-eu-ets-2
**Re-check window: Q2 2026** (pending formal legislative approval).

---

**ETS2 Start Date Formally Confirmed at 2028 — FACT, already resolved**
"Following a decision by the EU Council and the European Parliament in March 2026, the start date was postponed for one year, to 2028." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU ETS 2 factsheet. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/eu-emissions-trading-system-buildings-and-road-transport-eu-ets-2 "The postponement has been formally implemented by an EU regulation, which primarily sets out amendments to the European Climate Law." *Source: DEHSt, EU ETS 2 reporting phase (2024-2027), last updated 14 April 2026. https://www.dehst.de/EN/Topics/nEHS/EU-ETS-2/reporting-phase-2024-2026/eu-ets2_node.html No further date uncertainty exists for ETS2 full operationalisation.

---

*No content for the ReFuelEU Aviation SAF blend schedule section: the IATA ReFuelEU Aviation handbook source block was received in binary/PDF format and yielded no extractable text. SAF blend percentages and compliance deadlines from that regulation cannot be span-grounded from the provided source blocks. See New Sources Identified for the EUR-Lex entry as a retrieval lead.*

---

# Threshold Questions

## Decided Questions

**Q1: Is the Fit for 55 package fully adopted?**
Yes. All fifteen component instruments listed on the European Commission tracking page carry the status "Adopted" as of 31 October 2025. *Source: European Commission, Fit for 55: Delivering on the proposals. https://commission.europa.eu/topics/climate-action/delivering-european-green-deal/fit-55-delivering-proposals_en

**Q2: Does the workspace's road freight exposure fall under ETS2?**
The workspace is not a regulated entity under ETS2; the obligation sits upstream with fuel suppliers. Cost exposure is indirect through carrier rate pass-through. *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en

**Q3: When does ETS2 become fully operational?**
1 January 2028. The start date was formally confirmed by EU Council and Parliament in March 2026. *Source: DEHSt, EU ETS 2 reporting phase, last updated 14 April 2026. https://www.dehst.de/EN/Topics/nEHS/EU-ETS-2/reporting-phase-2024-2026/eu-ets2_node.html

**Q4: When does CBAM full compliance obligation apply?**
1 January 2026. *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target

**Q5: Does maritime ETS apply at 100% for intra-EU voyages from 2026?**
Yes. "The obligation to surrender allowances rising from 40% of verified emissions in 2024 to 100% in 2026. All emissions from intra-EU voyages and within EU ports will be covered by the ETS, and 50% of the emissions for journeys to or from a non-EU country." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target

## Questions Requiring Legal Confirmation

*Legal Confirmation Required:* whether a freight forwarder acting as indirect customs representative for an importer of CBAM-covered goods bears any procedural obligation (including pre-submission data provision) under CBAM Regulation (EU) 2023/956, or whether all CBAM obligations attach exclusively to the authorised declarant.

*Legal Confirmation Required:* whether a forwarder issuing its own bill of lading as NVOCC on intra-EU ocean voyages acquires any EU ETS maritime obligation, or whether the obligation remains exclusively with the vessel operator.

*Legal Confirmation Required:* whether any existing or forthcoming EU legislation under the Fit for 55 package imposes Scope 3 disclosure obligations on freight forwarders as service providers — as distinct from the CSRD which covers large undertakings meeting defined thresholds.

---

# Substantive Requirements

## EU ETS — Aviation Component

The EU ETS has applied to intra-EEA aviation since 2012. The 2023 Fit for 55 revisions "raise the linear reduction factor (LRF) from 2.2% to 4.3% from 2024-2027 and to 4.4% from 2028-2030." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target A faster-declining cap means fewer free allowances available to airlines over time, increasing the cost of intra-EEA aviation. *Analytical inference:* For the workspace, which uses air as its primary transport mode, this translates into structurally rising carrier costs on intra-EEA airfreight legs as free allocation declines.

**Penalty for non-compliance (aviation ETS):** *Specific penalty provision for aviation ETS non-compliance not extractable as a verbatim span from the provided source blocks as of this date.* Covered entities under the broader ETS framework face financial penalties for non-surrender; the specific aviation-ETS penalty quantum is a research gap in the source pool provided. *Legal Confirmation Required:* the operative penalty provision for aviation ETS non-compliance and whether any obligation flows to the freight forwarder level.

## EU ETS — Maritime Component

"The reform entails the phasing in of maritime sector emissions into the EU ETS, with the obligation to surrender allowances rising from 40% of verified emissions in 2024 to 100% in 2026." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target

The obligation covers: "all emissions from intra-EU voyages and within EU ports...and 50% of the emissions for journeys to or from a non-EU country." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target

*Analytical inference:* For the workspace's ocean freight on trans-Atlantic or Asia-Europe lanes, the 50% rule means ocean carriers will embed ETS cost into approximately half of voyage emissions on non-EU legs — a structural surcharge that will be present on every EU-touching ocean shipment from 2026.

## ETS2 — Road Transport Fuels

ETS2 covers "emissions from fuels used for combustion in buildings, road transport and additional sectors (mainly small industry not covered by the existing EU ETS)." *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en

"The ETS2 cap will be set to bring emissions down by 42% by 2030 compared to 2005 levels." *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en

"Regulated entities covered by the ETS2 are required to hold a greenhouse gas emissions permit by 1 January 2025, as well as an approved monitoring plan." *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en

"Every year, regulated entities must submit an emissions report by 30 April for the emissions of the previous year. From 2026, the data for a given year will have to be verified by an accredited verifier. From 2028, once annual verified emissions are reported, regulated entities will have to surrender the equivalent number of allowances by 31 May of the next year." *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en

**ETS2 Penalty:** "Covered entities must pay an excess emissions penalty of EUR 100 (USD 113), adjusted for inflation with 2013 as the base year, for each tCO2 emitted for which no allowance has been surrendered, in addition to buying and surrendering the equivalent number of allowances. The name of the non-compliant entity is also made public." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU ETS 2 factsheet. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/eu-emissions-trading-system-buildings-and-road-transport-eu-ets-2

*Analytical inference:* The workspace is not itself a regulated entity under ETS2. The EUR 100/tCO2 penalty applies to fuel suppliers. The workspace's exposure is through carrier rate pass-through, not direct penalty risk.

**ETS2 Price Stability Mechanism:** "During the first two years the ETS2 is operational, if the price of allowances exceeds €45 (in 2020 prices, i.e. adjusted for inflation), additional allowances may be released from the ETS2 market stability reserve to address excessive price increases." *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en

**ETS2 Road Transport Cost Estimate:** "Analysts estimate ETS2 could add roughly €0.10–€0.12 per litre of diesel by 2030." *Source: CFP Energy, ETS2 Road Transport Overview, 29 October 2025. https://www.cfp.energy/en/insights/ets2-road-transport-overview This is an industry analyst estimate, not a Commission-published figure. *Industry interpretation:* At this per-litre range, a road freight carrier operating 150,000 km/year at 30 litres/100 km would face approximately €4,500–€5,400 per vehicle per year in additional fuel cost — a cost that carriers operating on thin road freight margins are structurally incentivised to pass through.

## CBAM — Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism

"The CBAM phase-in plan gradually ceases the free allocation of EU ETS allowances over a nine-year period (from 2026 to 2034) for sectors covered by CBAM." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target

**Covered sectors:** cement, aluminium, fertilisers, electricity, hydrogen, iron and steel, and some precursors and downstream products. *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target

**Full compliance obligation:** 1 January 2026. Importers surrender CBAM certificates equivalent to embedded emissions. *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU adopts landmark ETS reforms, 3 May 2023. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/news/eu-adopts-landmark-ets-reforms-and-new-policies-meet-2030-target

*Specific CBAM penalty provision for non-surrendered certificates not available as a verbatim span from the provided source blocks as of this date.* This is an explicit research gap; the DEHSt source references CBAM sanctioning as a topic area but does not provide the penalty quantum in the extracted text.

## CO2 Standards for Cars and Vans

Regulation (EU) 2023/851, published in OJ L 110 on 25 April 2023, amends CO2 standards "as regards strengthening the CO2 emission performance standards for new passenger cars and new light commercial vehicles in line with the Union's increased climate ambition." *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/851. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R0851 This regulation applies to vehicle manufacturers and type-approval authorities, not directly to freight forwarders. *Analytical inference:* The workspace experiences this regulation's effect through the accelerating transition in last-mile delivery vehicles operated by road freight carriers — particularly relevant for European road secondary-mode operations.

## AFIR — Alternative Fuels Infrastructure

"Main EU ports (TEN-T ports) are required to provide a minimum shore-side electricity supply for seagoing container ships and seagoing passenger ships over 5,000 GT as of January 2030." *Source: Bureau Veritas Marine & Offshore, Alternative Fuels Infrastructure Regulation. https://marine-offshore.bureauveritas.com/sustainability/fit-55/alternative-fuels-infrastructure-regulation

"TEN-T maritime ports should provide an appropriate number of refuelling points for liquified methane by January 2025." *Source: Bureau Veritas Marine & Offshore, Alternative Fuels Infrastructure Regulation. https://marine-offshore.bureauveritas.com/sustainability/fit-55/alternative-fuels-infrastructure-regulation

*Analytical inference:* For the workspace's ocean freight legs through TEN-T ports, AFIR's shore power and alternative fuel infrastructure targets directly enable FuelEU Maritime compliance by ocean carriers, which underpins the availability of lower-emission ocean freight options on EU routes. The workspace can credibly reference AFIR as part of its explanation of why EU-touching ocean routes are becoming structurally greener over the 2025–2030 period.

---

# Product-Specific Compliance Status

*No content for this section as of this date: the workspace does not sell products under its own name in the Fit for 55 regulated goods categories (cement, aluminium, fertilisers, electricity, hydrogen, iron and steel). Product-specific CBAM compliance obligations rest with importers of record in covered goods categories, not with the freight forwarder.*

---

# Registration and Reporting Obligations

## ETS2 — Regulated Fuel Suppliers

"Regulated entities covered by the ETS2 are required to hold a greenhouse gas emissions permit by 1 January 2025." *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en "Covered entities must have approved monitoring plans in place as part of their GHG permits." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU ETS 2 factsheet. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/eu-emissions-trading-system-buildings-and-road-transport-eu-ets-2

"By the end of April 2025, covered entities submitted their first emission reports for their 2024 covered emissions." *Source: ICAP Carbon Action Partnership, EU ETS 2 factsheet. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets/eu-emissions-trading-system-buildings-and-road-transport-eu-ets-2

These obligations apply to fuel suppliers, not to the workspace. *Analytical inference:* However, road haulage carriers procured by the workspace are likely customers of fuel suppliers subject to ETS2 MRV, meaning they will have ETS2-related emissions data available from 2025 onward. The workspace may be able to obtain Scope 3 transport emissions data from carriers that includes ETS2 embedded costs — a future capability relevant to CSRD reporting if the workspace meets CSRD thresholds.

## CBAM — Importer Registration

The CBAM transitional period ran from 1 October 2023 through 31 December 2025. Importers of covered goods are required to register as authorised CBAM declarants for the full compliance regime from 1 January 2026. *Source: DEHSt, CBAM Definitive Regime from 2026 (page reference from DEHSt site navigation). https://www.dehst.de/EN/Topics/nEHS/EU-ETS-2/reporting-phase-2024-2026/eu-ets2_node.html The workspace should confirm that clients importing CBAM-covered goods have completed or are completing declarant authorisation.

*Specific published format or procedural template for CBAM declarant authorisation not available as a verbatim span from the provided source blocks as of this date.*

---

# Operational System Requirements

## Road Freight Cost Tracking — ETS2 Pass-Through

**Scope:** All road freight procurement on EU Member State routes.
**Deadline:** ETS2 full operationalisation 1 January 2028; carrier hedging and pre-pricing behaviour beginning 2026.
**Gap from current baseline:** Workspaces operating road freight cost tracking on a rate-per-shipment or tonnage basis without a carbon cost line item cannot distinguish ETS2 pass-through surcharges from general rate inflation. This gap will become commercially significant when ETS2 surcharges appear in carrier invoices from 2026 onward.
**Action:** Build a separate ETS2 cost line into road freight rate cards and reporting; request from road carriers their ETS2 cost modelling for 2026–2028. *Analytical inference:* Clients operating Scope 3 freight accounting will eventually ask the workspace to attribute carbon cost disaggregated by mode; ETS2 line-item tracking is the prerequisite.

## Ocean ETS Surcharge Validation

**Scope:** All ocean freight on EU-touching routes (intra-EU and EU extra-EU).
**Deadline:** 100% intra-EU obligation from 1 January 2026 (already in force).
**Gap:** Workspaces receiving carrier ETS surcharges as a bundled line item without breakdown of 100% vs 50% obligation structure cannot verify correct billing or pass through accurate costs to clients.
**Action:** Require ocean carrier partners to provide ETS surcharge methodology documentation distinguishing intra-EU and extra-EU voyage obligations; validate against the 100%/50% structure established by the 2023 ETS revision.

## CBAM Documentation for Covered Cargo

**Scope:** Shipments containing goods in CBAM-covered categories.
**Deadline:** 1 January 2026.
**Gap:** Workspaces without a systematic process to identify CBAM-covered goods at booking stage cannot provide timely documentation support to importers, and cannot protect themselves from last-minute compliance escalations.
**Action:** Implement a cargo screening step at booking for CBAM-covered commodity codes; prepare a standard client advisory confirming that CBAM certificate obligations sit with the importer of record, not the forwarder.

## Carrier Emissions Data Request

**Scope:** Air, road, and ocean carrier relationships.
**Action:** In carrier RFPs and annual reviews, request ETS/ETS2/SAF/FuelEU emissions data and compliance cost itemisation. This positions the workspace to support client Scope 3 reporting and CSRD-driven supply chain disclosure requirements.

---

# Exemptions and Edge Cases

*No content for this section as of this date: the Fit for 55 instruments affecting the workspace's operations (ETS, ETS2, CBAM, AFIR) do not create exemptions applicable to freight forwarding operations as identified in the provided source blocks. Exemptions that exist in the ETS (e.g., small emitter simplified procedures) apply to regulated entities (aircraft operators, vessel operators, fuel suppliers) not to freight forwarders. No sourced exemption provision applicable to the workspace's role was identified.*

---

# Adjacent Industry Research and Alternatives

## ETS2 Futures Market Development

In August 2025, the first EU ETS2 futures trade was executed on the EEX exchange. *Source: CFP Energy, ETS2 Road Transport Overview, 29 October 2025. https://www.cfp.energy/en/insights/ets2-road-transport-overview *Analytical inference:* This development signals that financial hedging instruments for ETS2 allowance costs will be available to road freight carriers from 2026, enabling them to lock in carbon costs in advance. Carriers with sophisticated hedging strategies will have more predictable ETS2 cost pass-through compared to those relying on spot markets — a factor the workspace can use to evaluate carrier selection.

## Alternative Road Fuels — HVO as Carbon Cost Mitigation

*Industry interpretation:* CFP Energy notes that "hydrotreated vegetable oil (HVO), a drop-in bio-diesel substitute, can cut lifecycle CO2 emissions by up to 90%, dramatically reducing a fleet's exposure to carbon costs." *Source: CFP Energy, ETS2 Road Transport Overview, 29 October 2025. https://www.cfp.energy/en/insights/ets2-road-transport-overview This figure is a lifecycle estimate and subject to feedstock and methodology assumptions; it is cited as an industry interpretation, not a Commission-verified figure. *Analytical inference:* Carriers operating on HVO may carry lower ETS2 exposure, translating into lower surcharges for the workspace — a differentiator worth exploring in road carrier selection criteria.

## Social Climate Fund — Revenue Recycling for Infrastructure

"With an additional 25% co-financing from Member States, the Fund is expected to mobilise at least EUR 86.7 billion between 2026 and 2032." *Source: Climate Action, ETS2: buildings, road transport and additional sectors, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/carbon-markets/ets2-buildings-road-transport-and-additional-sectors_en The Social Climate Fund is directed at vulnerable households and micro-enterprises, not commercial freight. *Analytical inference:* The fund does support clean transport infrastructure investment by Member States, which over time improves the EV charging and alternative fuel refuelling environment that road carriers serving the workspace will use.

cl.claim_text: [effective_date] The Fit for 55 package is a collection of EU legislative instruments originally proposed by the European Commission on 14 July 2021.
All fifteen component instruments listed on the European Commission's tracking page carry the status 'Adopted' as of 31 October 2025.
Fuel suppliers will be required to surrender ETS2 allowances from 2028, but early ETS2 allowance auctions are planned for 2026 to allow suppliers to begin hedging.
[primary_deadline] The ETS maritime obligation reaches 100% of intra-EU voyage emissions and 50% of extra-EU voyage emissions in 2026.
Vessel operators (shipowners and managers) are the obligated parties under maritime ETS.
It will be fuel suppliers, rather than end consumers such as households or car users, that will be required to monitor and report their emissions under ETS2.
The communication explicitly identifies that 'carbon pricing instruments...raise revenues that can be reinvested to address energy poverty and mobility challenges for the vulnerable.'
The CBAM phase-out of free ETS allocation for covered sectors runs 'over a nine-year period (from 2026 to 2034).'
In July 2025, the Commission proposed an amendment to the EU Climate Law establishing 'an EU climate target for 2040 of a 90% reduction in net GHG emissions, compared to 1990 levels.'
'The Commission launched a public consultation in April 2025 to gather input on the integration of GHG removals...with a report expected by July 2026.'
All fifteen component instruments listed on the European Commission tracking page carry the status 'Adopted' as of 31 October 2025.
[primary_deadline] ETS2 full operational start date is 1 January 2028, formally confirmed by EU Council and Parliament in March 2026.
Maritime ETS applies at 100% for intra-EU voyages from 2026 and 50% for extra-EU voyages.
The 2023 Fit for 55 revisions raise the linear reduction factor from 2.2% to 4.3% from 2024-2027 and to 4.4% from 2028-2030.
The maritime ETS obligation covers all emissions from intra-EU voyages and within EU ports, and 50% of the emissions for journeys to or from a non-EU country.
Regulated entities covered by the ETS2 are required to hold a greenhouse gas emissions permit by 1 January 2025.
By the end of April 2025, covered entities submitted their first emission reports for their 2024 covered emissions.
[primary_deadline] ETS2 full operationalisation deadline is 1 January 2028; carrier hedging and pre-pricing behaviour beginning 2026.
The EU Climate Law establishes a binding Union domestic reduction target for net greenhouse gas emissions of at least 55% compared to 1990 levels by 2030.
[effective_date] The earliest ETS revisions applied from 1 January 2024.
ETS2 futures trading has already begun (first EU ETS2 futures trade executed on EEX in August 2025).
[primary_deadline] CBAM full compliance obligation applies from 1 January 2026.
Importers in sectors covered by CBAM will be required to surrender newly created CBAM certificates equivalent to the embedded emissions of their products.

The Commission's communication describes the Fit for 55 package as 'a set of interconnected proposals that together deliver our ambition' and characterises the policy mix as 'a careful balance between pricing, targets, standards and support measures.'
The communication describes the Social Climate Fund as providing '€72.2 billion in current prices for the period 2025-2032 in the EU budget from the new Emissions Trading System.'
The CBAM regulation was published in the Official Journal on 16 May 2023 (OJ L 130, pp. 52–104).
The proposal has been submitted 'to the European Parliament (Parliament) and the European Council (Council) for discussion and adoption under the ordinary legislative procedure.'

The Commission proposed further changes to the ETS2 market stability reserve in November 2025 'to support a smooth start of the EU ETS 2 and accelerate early investments into sectoral decarbonization.'
ETS2 obligation sits upstream with fuel suppliers.
[primary_deadline] CBAM full compliance obligation applies from 1 January 2026.
[penalty_summary] Penalty summary for ETS2 non-compliance: covered entities must pay an excess emissions penalty.
The reform entails the phasing in of maritime sector emissions into the EU ETS, with the obligation to surrender allowances rising from 40% of verified emissions in 2024 to 100% in 2026.
[penalty_summary] Penalty for aviation ETS non-compliance: specific penalty provision not extractable as verbatim span from provided source blocks.
Covered entities must have approved monitoring plans in place as part of their GHG permits.

The CBAM transitional period ran from 1 October 2023 through 31 December 2025; importers of covered goods are required to register as authorised CBAM declarants for the full compliance regime from 1 January 2026.
100% intra-EU maritime ETS obligation is in force from 1 January 2026.
In August 2025, the first EU ETS2 futures trade was executed on the EEX exchange.
AFIR Regulation 2023/1804 entered into force on 12 October 2023.
ETS2 regulated entities were required to hold GHG emissions permit and approved monitoring plan from 1 January 2025.
[primary_deadline] ETS2 full operationalisation — allowance surrender obligation begins 1 January 2028.
DEHSt EU ETS 2 reporting phase page was last updated 14 April 2026.
CBAM documentation compliance deadline for covered cargo is 1 January 2026.
'With an additional 25% co-financing from Member States, the Fund is expected to mobilise at least EUR 86.7 billion between 2026 and 2032.'
AFIR deadline for submission of draft national policy frameworks by Member States was 31 December 2024.
AFIR requires TEN-T ports to provide shore power for container ships over 5,000 GT by January 2030.
Regulation (EU) 2023/956 establishing CBAM was adopted by the European Parliament and Council on 10 May 2023.
